summary:
A newborn presenting with facial and limb vesicular eruptions suggests HSV infection as it commonly affects this age group; treatment includes using acyclovir topically due to its effectiveness against such infections.
To refine further while keeping within two concise sentences:

The presentation strongly indicates an HSV infection given typical neonatal involvement sites like the face and extremities; appropriate management involves applying acyclovir locally because of its efficacy against these types of viral skin conditions.
